properties.summary: Product-facing founder workspace for the Decision & Capability OS and the command surface for building reusable company systems.

Core Job Studio department and company context inside Startup Intelligence OS for work-history retrieval, AI strategist enablement, training operations, opportunity intelligence, and cross-company context bridging.

execution_tracks.name: Job Studio Training Factory
execution_tracks.description: Build and run repeatable training packets, facilitation guides, handouts, examples, and evaluation loops for Mike Job Studio.

execution_tracks.name: AI Strategist Enablement
execution_tracks.description: Train strategists on what AI is, how to use it well, and when to transition into structured Ellen-led workflows.

execution_tracks.name: Ellen and Gen Chat Enablement
execution_tracks.description: Transition strategists from general AI usage into Ellen in Gen Chat for Oracle Health strategic workflows and deliverable production.

execution_tracks.name: Work Memory and Retrieval
execution_tracks.description: Retrieve grounded work-history context, thread recaps, and source maps from the attached Job Studio corpus.

execution_tracks.name: Writing Style and Work Product Intelligence
execution_tracks.description: Recover tone, persuasion patterns, and reusable structures from Mike-authored material.

execution_tracks.name: Resume and Positioning Packets
execution_tracks.description: Build resume, cover letter, LinkedIn, and role-positioning packets from grounded Job Studio evidence.

execution_tracks.name: Interview and Recruiter Prep
execution_tracks.description: Prepare interview, recruiter, and hiring-manager packets by combining personal memory with target-company research.

execution_tracks.name: Opportunity Briefs and Application Strategy
execution_tracks.description: Evaluate roles, companies, and application paths using Job Studio memory and explicit strategy framing.

execution_tracks.name: Linked Company Context
execution_tracks.description: Bridge Job Studio memory with company-specific corpora such as Oracle Health when the ask depends on both.

properties.objective: Add the root workspace contract, Jake front door, and file-backed operating kernel without breaking the current Susan runtime.

nodes.name: Job Studio Maturity and Training Factory

properties.objective: Build Job Studio as a sixth core department with a full zero-to-ten maturity roadmap, a multi-gigabyte training and behavioral-science corpus, and explicit agentic training protocols.
deliverables: full zero-to-ten maturity roadmap across all departments and system layers
Job Studio training factory roadmap and protocol stack
resumable corpus extraction and ingest pipeline

nodes.name: Adopt Customer User Studio as a core Startup OS capability

properties.decision_statement: Establish a Customer User Studio capability that runs adaptive customer agents, captures preferences, and informs roadmap decisions across applications.
properties.why_now: Upcoming multi-application rollout requires scalable customer simulation and beta feedback loops to de-risk releases and improve prioritization.
constraints: Preserve Susan runtime behavior under susan-team-architect/backend.
Keep changes additive and file-backed under .startup-os.
Ensure every roadmap recommendation links to session evidence artifacts.

options.name: Lean validation layer
options.summary: Scripted browser tests with bug logging and limited feedback extraction.

options.name: Core customer user studio
options.summary: Persona/scenario schemas, adaptive agents, preference capture, and roadmap intelligence mapping.

options.name: Autonomous customer twin platform
options.summary: Population simulation, longitudinal memory, causal forecasting, and autonomous experimentation.

assumptions: Synthetic agents and beta cohorts can be reconciled through shared evidence schema.
Weekly decision-room cadence is sufficient for roadmap updates in phase 1.
Release quality gates can be introduced incrementally without blocking current delivery velocity.

risks.name: Synthetic-user bias
risks.mitigation: Blend synthetic evidence with beta cohort signals and track divergence score.

risks.name: Signal overload
risks.mitigation: Use severity and confidence scoring with strict ranking thresholds.

risks.name: Governance gaps
risks.mitigation: Define beta consent boundaries and release gate ownership before rollout.

next_actions: Add customer persona/scenario/session schemas and templates.
Stand up first scenario pack for onboarding, activation, and retention workflows.
Generate weekly roadmap intelligence artifact from session evidence.
Define beta cohort playbook and release gate policy in wave 1.

nodes.name: Adopt a Phase A Decision & Capability OS overlay at repo root

properties.decision_statement: Add a root-level Jake front door and workspace contract while preserving the current Susan runtime untouched.
properties.why_now: The repo has diverged between a founder resource hub identity and a Susan-centered runtime, creating context drift at root.
constraints: Do not break susan-team-architect runtime behavior.
Do not rename or remove current Susan commands or MCP server.
Keep the terminal as the primary execution surface.

options.name: Additive root overlay
options.summary: Introduce Jake, .startup-os, and launch helpers at repo root while leaving Susan backend intact.

options.name: Full Susan-to-Jake rewrite
options.summary: Replace root and backend identity in one pass.

options.name: Plan-only import
options.summary: Import the packet but avoid active runtime changes.

assumptions: Existing Susan runtime remains the operational source of truth.
Root instructions are the correct place to switch the conversational front door to Jake.
reversal_criteria: If root Jake instructions create conflicts with existing Susan sessions or MCP behavior, reduce Jake to launcher-only and keep Susan as default.
next_actions: Import the plan docs.
Add root workspace files and launchers.
Reconcile root CLAUDE instructions.

nodes.name: Advance Customer User Studio from core baseline to world-class L5 maturity

properties.decision_statement: Adopt a phased L1-L5 world-class maturity model for Customer User Studio, with explicit release gates and roadmap intelligence accountability.
properties.why_now: Core scaffolding exists; the next value frontier is reliable adaptive coverage, beta alignment, and predictive roadmap prioritization before multi-app scale accelerates.
constraints: Keep Susan runtime behavior unchanged under susan-team-architect/backend.
Keep Customer User Studio artifacts additive and file-backed under .startup-os.
Require evidence lineage for all roadmap recommendations.

options.name: Maintain core-only mode
options.summary: Keep deterministic testing and basic preference capture without predictive or autonomous layers.

options.name: Phase to world-class L5
options.summary: Implement a staged progression through operational, adaptive, predictive, and autonomous maturity.

options.name: Jump directly to autonomous platform
options.summary: Build full population simulation and autonomous scenario generation immediately.

assumptions: Phase-based delivery reduces risk while preserving momentum.
World-class maturity requires synthetic and beta evidence reconciliation on every release cycle.
Decision-room cadence can absorb weekly ranked opportunity packets.

risks.name: Over-complexity before adoption
risks.mitigation: Gate scope by maturity level with measurable exit criteria.

risks.name: Recommendation trust erosion
risks.mitigation: Track hit rate of shipped recommendations vs KPI outcomes.

risks.name: Governance bottlenecks
risks.mitigation: Assign single-threaded ownership for triage, waivers, and release gate decisions.

next_actions: Define L1-L5 exit criteria and owners for each level.
Launch top 25 deterministic journeys and nightly run cadence.
Add synthetic-vs-beta alignment reporting and release waiver protocol.
Stand up predictive ranking model pilot on one flagship application.

nodes.name: Build the Job Studio Training Factory corpus and protocol layer

properties.decision_statement: Build a local-first, large-scale Job Studio Training Factory corpus program with extraction, manifests, locators, agent access rules, and writeback paths.
properties.why_now: The training factory now depends on gigabytes of real corpus material, explicit access protocols, and reusable examples. Without that layer, the department stays presentation-rich but evidence-thin.
constraints: Start with local corpora and local repo harvests before relying on external live crawls.
Keep personal memory and company delivery memory namespace-separated.
External live crawl jobs depend on the relevant API keys being present.

options.name: Manual document lookup only
options.summary: Leave the source material on disk and rely on manual file inspection case by case.

options.name: Local-first extraction plus live-refresh manifests
options.summary: Normalize local corpora now, add ingest and locator paths, and wire official manifests for live refresh when keys are available.

assumptions: The Oracle desktop mirror is the fastest high-value corpus to operationalize.
Local adjacent repos are valid and useful training inputs.
Agents need explicit data-access protocols and locators to use the corpus consistently.
reversal_criteria: If the extracted corpus proves too noisy to use, narrow the source tiers and emphasize named example libraries first.
next_actions: Build the training corpus extraction script and run it.
Publish the training factory locator and protocol docs.
Add live-refresh scrape manifests for official docs and known sources.
Add workspace-local skills for harvesting, evaluation, and training production.

nodes.name: Build a MyJobStudio training hub directly inside the Oracle Health AI Enablement repository for tonight's training-site need

properties.decision_statement: Stand up a no-build static training hub in the Oracle Health AI Enablement repo, backed by MyJobStudio session packets and a reusable Oracle Gen Chat access prompt, so training materials can be built tonight without waiting on a framework setup.
properties.why_now: The Oracle training content exists as markdown and facilitation guides, but there is no navigable site surface. The fastest reliable move tonight is a static hub that can be served locally and expanded immediately.
constraints: Preserve the existing Oracle Health AI Enablement repository structure.
Avoid a heavy framework or package install tonight.
Keep the site usable from local HTTP hosting with minimal setup.

options.name: Keep working from markdown files only
options.summary: Continue building from raw markdown without a site surface.

options.name: Build a static training hub now
options.summary: Add a direct-access prompt, MyJobStudio session packets, and a static HTML training site in the Oracle repo.

assumptions: The training site needs to exist tonight more than it needs a full app framework.
Existing Oracle workshop guides for Sessions 4-8 are strong enough to act as the applied source spine.
Track C needs new MyJobStudio session packets because the existing workshop arc does not fully cover strategist AI foundations.
reversal_criteria: If the team needs LMS features, auth, or hosted distribution later, migrate the content into a fuller app surface after the static hub proves the information architecture.
next_actions: Expand the session packets into facilitator guides, handouts, and deck files.
Add slide and handout build pipelines for each session.
Connect the training site to a hosted preview when distribution is ready.

nodes.name: Build Session 1 as an editable PowerPoint deck with an adult-learning-first design system for Job Studio

properties.decision_statement: Build Session 1 as an editable PowerPoint deck in the Oracle Health AI Enablement repository, using a reusable executive-workshop visual system and visible adult-learning layers so the rest of Track C can scale from the same template.
properties.why_now: Session 1 needs to be usable immediately in a training setting, and the fastest durable move is an editable `.pptx` with facilitator notes, a defined visual system, and real-work learning structure.
constraints: Preserve Oracle Health visual standards while making the deck feel like a training system rather than a sales deck.
Keep the output editable in Microsoft PowerPoint and rebuildable from source.
Embed adult-learning layers directly into the deck structure instead of leaving them in a separate facilitation note.

options.name: Convert the markdown outline into plain slides
options.summary: Build a straightforward deck from the existing slide script with minimal design or learning architecture changes.

options.name: Build a reusable Job Studio workshop deck system
options.summary: Create a PowerPoint-native Session 1 deck with a visual system, facilitator notes, adult-learning layers, and rebuildable source.

assumptions: Session 1 should establish the look and feel for the rest of Track C.
Speaker notes inside PowerPoint are more useful tonight than keeping facilitation logic only in markdown.
Adult learners will respond better to a visible session arc built around relevance, mental model, practice, reflection, and transfer.
reversal_criteria: If a different brand or delivery surface becomes required, keep the content model and rebuild the deck system on a new visual shell rather than rewriting the curriculum.
next_actions: Build Session 2 and Session 3 on the same deck system.
Add learner handout and facilitator guide parity for Session 1.
Reuse the same visual shell across Track B once Ellen transition materials move into PowerPoint.

nodes.name: Connect Customer User Studio artifacts to Susan Intelligence OS backend data surface

properties.decision_statement: Establish a repeatable sync pathway that pushes Customer User Studio artifacts from `.startup-os` into `susan-team-architect/backend/data/startup_os/customer_user_studio`.
properties.why_now: Customer User Studio must be accessible to Susan Intelligence OS runtime workflows, not only stored in root workspace artifacts.
constraints: Preserve Susan runtime behavior under guarded backend runtime directories.
Keep the integration additive and file-backed.
Maintain Jake as primary operator surface.

options.name: Keep artifacts only in .startup-os
options.summary: No backend sync; Susan backend remains disconnected from Customer User Studio artifact stream.

options.name: Add Jake + backend script sync bridge
options.summary: Add `bin/jake customer-studio push-susan` plus backend sync script and publish synced artifacts under backend data path.

assumptions: Susan workflows can consume file-backed YAML artifacts from backend data path.
Sync cadence can be operator-triggered initially and automated later.

risks.name: stale backend mirror
risks.mitigation: run sync after seed/report updates and capture timestamped sync-index.

risks.name: schema drift between root and backend copies
risks.mitigation: keep canonical schemas in .startup-os and validate before pushing.

next_actions: Add scheduled sync mode for nightly refresh.
Wire backend ingest job to consume synced artifacts.
Expand source set to include Brave/Exa/Jina/Firecrawl/Apify manifests.

nodes.name: Adopt event-driven architecture for data pipeline

properties.context: Our current batch processing creates 4-hour lag. Moving to Kafka-based streaming could reduce latency to minutes. Team has some Kafka experience.
assumptions: Kafka cluster can be provisioned in 1 week
Team can ramp on Kafka Streams in 2 weeks

options.title: Go forward: Adopt event-driven architecture for data pipeline
options.description: Commit to executing the proposed direction. Our current batch processing creates 4-hour lag. Moving to Kafka-based streaming could reduce latency to minutes. Team h. Allocate resources now, set a 2-week checkpoint, and drive toward the first measurable milestone.

options.title: Alternative approach: scoped experiment for Adopt event-driven architecture for data pipeline
options.description: Rather than a full commitment, run a time-boxed experiment. Our current batch processing creates 4-hour lag. Moving to Kafka-based streaming could reduce latency to minutes. Team h. Limit scope to a single cohort or feature surface, measure leading indicators for 1 week, then decide whether to scale up, pivot, or stop.

options.title: Do nothing / defer
options.description: Preserve the status quo and defer this decision. Revisit in the next planning cycle once more evidence is available. Accept the opportunity cost of delayed action in exchange for reduced execution risk and resource preservation.

nodes.name: Enable direct iPhone automation for workout app reconnaissance

properties.decision_statement: Use a real-device XCTest harness to automate the installed workout app on the iPhone itself instead of driving the macOS iPhone Mirroring window.
properties.why_now: Mirroring-based exploration moves the Mac cursor and blocks normal desktop work. Direct device automation preserves the user's mouse workflow while giving us a path to repeatable, scriptable app reconnaissance.
constraints: The target app source code is not available.
The target app is a third-party installed iPhone app identified as `com.sbs.train`.
Real-device automation requires Apple signing and provisioning for the probe runner.
The existing Susan runtime and root workspace must remain unaffected.

options.name: Keep using the mirrored macOS window
options.summary: Continue coordinate-driving the iPhone Mirroring app from the Mac.

options.name: Build a direct XCTest probe
options.summary: Create a minimal signed host app and UI test runner that activates the installed app by bundle identifier on the phone itself.

assumptions: The bundle identifier `com.sbs.train` is the correct target app.
The connected phone remains trusted, development-enabled, and reachable from Xcode.
The Apple Development identity on this Mac belongs to team `3343A3GQAT`.
Once Xcode account access is restored, provisioning can be created without changing the probe code.
reversal_criteria: If Xcode account access cannot be restored or provisioning cannot be created, fall back to hybrid analysis using background device tooling plus user-driven walkthroughs.
next_actions: Sign into Xcode Accounts for team `3343A3GQAT`, or restore existing provisioning profiles.
Rerun `./apps/ios-device-probe/run_probe.sh`.
Extend the probe beyond tab navigation into screen-level exploration and screenshot harvesting.
Combine direct UI automation with `rvictl` network capture for backend inference.

nodes.name: Launch an all-department maturity gap closure scrape wave across the lowest-scoring departments and training systems

properties.decision_statement: Start a direct-URL, multi-manifest scrape wave to close evidence gaps for low-maturity departments, training evaluation, and official operating references across Startup Intelligence OS.
properties.why_now: The local and Oracle corpora are now materially extracted, but multiple departments remain low-maturity because their evidence layers are still thin or uneven. The next leverage point is targeted public-doc crawling, not more generalized collection.
constraints: Use direct URLs and known repositories; do not rely on search-generated manifests for this wave.
Preserve the Susan runtime and route new data through existing ingest surfaces.
Keep training-specific harvests in mike-job-studio and shared department intelligence in founder-intelligence-os.
Prefer official documentation, handbooks, standards bodies, and stable public sources.

options.name: Wait for later manual research
options.summary: Keep the current maturity map and fill gaps ad hoc when a department needs support.

options.name: Targeted all-department gap closure wave
options.summary: Run a defined set of manifests for the weakest departments, Job Studio training evaluation, and official operating references, then use the new evidence to lift maturity and protocols.

assumptions: The most important maturity gaps are in talent/org design, finance cadence, revenue/growth, trust/governance, data/decision science, and training evaluation.
Direct crawls over official docs and handbooks are strong enough to improve evidence quality without a search layer.
The current job-studio training corpus can absorb and benefit from added evaluation, AI foundations, and protocol references immediately.
reversal_criteria: If the crawl wave produces low-signal data or weak chunk quality, reduce scope to higher-signal sources and raise the threshold for new manifests.
If the added corpus creates retrieval noise without improving routed outputs, tighten data types and named source bundles before expanding further.
next_actions: Let the full resumable wave finish and roll the new counts into the latest summary.
Refresh the maturity dashboard after the full batch, not only targeted reruns.
Keep converting weak departments into reusable operating packs and scorecards.
Turn the new evidence into scorecards, role packets, cadence templates, and training assets.

nodes.name: Launch a stronger dataset and benchmark enrichment wave for Startup Intelligence OS and Job Studio

properties.decision_statement: Add stronger benchmark, evaluation, and synthetic-run review datasets through repeatable Susan manifests instead of relying only on the current department gap-closure set.
properties.why_now: The OS now has full contract coverage, but the next maturity lift depends on stronger benchmark libraries, richer evaluation datasets, and better synthetic-run review material for Susan's routed agents and Job Studio training work.
constraints: Prefer official docs, official repos, stable public reports, and durable handbook sources.
Keep founder-intelligence-os and mike-job-studio corpora separated by company_id.
Use repeatable manifests and runners so Susan's research agent can refresh the stack.

options.name: Keep using current manifests only
options.summary: Rely on the existing department and training manifests without adding a stronger benchmark/eval wave.

options.name: Add stronger benchmark and eval dataset wave
options.summary: Build a targeted source stack for benchmark cases, synthetic evaluation, and adult-learning evidence, then ingest it into Susan's knowledge base.

assumptions: Stronger benchmark and evaluation data will raise simulated maturity faster than adding more wrappers.
OpenAI, NIST, GitLab, SaaS benchmark reports, and adult-learning sources are strong enough to improve the benchmark library immediately.
Synthetic run reviews and eval test packs should be first-class proof objects in the benchmark layer.
reversal_criteria: If the stronger source stack produces noisy or low-signal chunks, narrow the manifests to the best-performing sources and lower breadth.
If the new data improves counts but not output quality, raise the threshold for what counts as benchmark-quality evidence.
next_actions: Promote the best-performing sources into maintained department benchmark libraries.
Attach the new `studio_evals` corpus to synthetic-run review and Monte Carlo harnesses.
Use the new learning-science layer to improve Track C and training facilitator-review loops.
Extend the stronger dataset wave with more official benchmark providers only if output quality keeps improving.

nodes.name: Normalize provider key aliases and keep live secrets in ignored local storage

properties.decision_statement: Use the ignored backend `.env` file as the live secret store, keep `.env.example` as the tracked template, and normalize provider alias handling in code so existing keys work without duplicating secrets across files.
properties.why_now: The scrape, ingest, and training-factory stack depends on multiple external providers. Keys already existed locally, but several scripts expected different variable names and therefore silently missed working credentials.
constraints: Do not commit live secrets into git.
Keep one local source of truth for active credentials.
Make provider health visible through a non-secret healthcheck.

options.name: Store secrets directly in tracked repo files
options.summary: Put active keys in committed config so every script sees them.

options.name: Use ignored local env plus tracked template and alias normalization
options.summary: Keep secrets local and ignored, track only templates and health tooling, and update code to accept the real env names already in use.

assumptions: A single ignored local env file is safer than duplicating secrets across the repo.
The main operational problem is alias drift, not total key absence.
OpenAI may still require a manual dashboard pull if no local key exists.
reversal_criteria: If a dedicated secret manager is introduced later, move the live source of truth out of `.env` and keep only adapters in the repo.
next_actions: Keep using the provider healthcheck before major ingest or scrape runs.
Add the missing OpenAI key to the ignored backend env file when available.
Let the running Job Studio corpus ingest finish and verify stored counts afterward.

nodes.name: Add executable Jake workflows for Customer User Studio seeding, validation, and reporting

properties.decision_statement: Implement a terminal-first execution surface for Customer User Studio via `bin/jake customer-studio` commands that seed artifacts, validate contracts, and generate ranked opportunities.
properties.why_now: Prior iterations were documentation-heavy; we need runnable workflows that produce evidence artifacts and roadmap inputs immediately.
constraints: Preserve Susan runtime behavior under susan-team-architect/backend.
Keep workflows additive and file-backed in .startup-os.
Maintain Jake as the default root front door.

options.name: Keep documentation-only studio
options.summary: Continue with capability/docs and no executable customer-studio command surface.

options.name: Add Jake customer-studio execution surface
options.summary: Add seed/validate/report commands and starter artifacts to make customer studio runnable now.

assumptions: Lightweight file-backed operations are sufficient for phase-1 execution.
Teams will iterate scenario and persona packs after seeded starter artifacts exist.

risks.name: Low fidelity scoring in early report generation
risks.mitigation: Evolve scoring weights in roadmap spec as more session evidence accumulates.

risks.name: Artifact sprawl
risks.mitigation: Keep outputs in a dedicated customer-user-studio artifact subtree with validation checks.

next_actions: Expand seeded journeys to top 25 real customer workflows.
Integrate report output into weekly decision-room packet.
Add CI check for customer-studio validate command.

nodes.name: Operationalize a machine-readable 10-state verification matrix and visual dashboard for all Startup Intelligence OS sections and departments

properties.decision_statement: Add a machine-readable verification matrix, department source-stack manifests, and a visual maturity dashboard so every core section and department has explicit 10/10 requirements for data sources and capabilities, current proof paths, and visible gap states.
properties.why_now: The OS already has maturity language, but not a rigorous verification surface. Without an explicit matrix and dashboard, the team can talk about maturity without proving it.
constraints: Preserve the existing department registry as the canonical wrapper layer.
Keep the verification system file-backed and reviewable in the repo.
Distinguish between defined source stacks and fully verified 10/10 operating maturity.

options.name: Keep maturity in prose only
options.summary: Continue using narrative maturity artifacts and department YAML files without a machine-readable verification surface.

options.name: Add a verification matrix and dashboard
options.summary: Create a machine-readable maturity contract for sections and departments, add missing source manifests, and render a dashboard from actual proof paths.

assumptions: Visible proof is more useful than another purely narrative roadmap.
Source-stack definition is a necessary precursor to full 10/10 data maturity.
Operator visibility itself is one of the missing capabilities for several departments.
reversal_criteria: If the matrix becomes too heavy to maintain, collapse it into a smaller contract with only the most decision-useful data and capability gates.
next_actions: Execute the expanded department gap-closure wave using the new manifests.
Update current scores after the next source refresh completes.
Add maturity drift alerts and section-level simulated summaries to the operator console.

nodes.name: Operationalize a zero-to-ten maturity system for all departments, studios, tools, and operating layers

properties.decision_statement: Move Startup Intelligence OS to one explicit 0-10 maturity system that defines what full maturity looks like for departments, data systems, protocols, tools, and operator surfaces.
properties.why_now: Department and capability growth is outpacing shared standards. Without a single maturity language, prioritization, roadmaping, and operator visibility stay inconsistent.
constraints: Preserve the existing Susan six-lens model as the governing lens set.
Keep the maturity system legible in file-backed artifacts and operator surfaces.
Make current-state visuals part of the maturity process, not an optional afterthought.

options.name: Keep mixed maturity scales
options.summary: Leave departments, capabilities, and assessments on different implicit scales and reconcile manually.

options.name: One explicit 0-10 system
options.summary: Standardize all major operating surfaces on a visible 0-10 maturity model with explicit 10-state definitions.

assumptions: A shared maturity language improves sequencing and decision quality.
Departments, tools, protocols, and data systems all need the same maturity frame, not only capabilities.
The system will be more useful if "10" is concrete and operator-visible.
reversal_criteria: If the 0-10 model creates noise without improving roadmap decisions, collapse back to a smaller set of maturity checkpoints.
next_actions: Publish the zero-to-ten maturity system artifact.
Align department records to the 0-10 scale.
Add the missing department packs to the registry.
Reflect the maturity visual in operator-facing artifacts.

nodes.name: Publish Customer User Studio outputs across repo, Susan backend, and local-drive mirror

properties.decision_statement: Add a unified publish workflow to generate report artifacts, sync into Susan backend data path, and mirror artifacts to a specified local-drive directory.
properties.why_now: Operators requested explicit push behavior across repository workspace, Susan Intelligence OS local backend folder, and local-drive destinations.
constraints: Preserve Susan runtime behavior and only add files under backend data/scripts surfaces.
Keep terminal-first workflow through bin/jake.

options.name: Separate manual commands
options.summary: Run report + push-susan + local copy manually each time.

options.name: Unified publish command
options.summary: Add `bin/jake customer-studio publish [local_drive_path]` to execute end-to-end publish and emit manifest.

assumptions: Local-drive path is operator-supplied and writable.
GitHub push remains dependent on configured git remote and credentials.

risks.name: publish path misconfiguration
risks.mitigation: emit publish manifest with resolved target paths for verification.

risks.name: stale remote publication
risks.mitigation: require explicit git push command and capture output in operator runbook.

next_actions: Add optional scheduled publish mode.
Add CI check for publish manifest generation.

nodes.name: Reframe Startup Intelligence OS as a department-grade Decision-and-Build OS with Job Studio as a sixth core department

properties.decision_statement: Reorganize Startup Intelligence OS around department-grade studios of excellence, a six-lens Susan maturity model, an agentic operator dashboard, and a fully built Job Studio as a sixth core department instead of continuing to expand a flat agent roster.
properties.why_now: The system now serves as application project, startup backend, developer environment, and capability foundry at the same time. Breadth is compounding faster than structure, which is creating duplicate surfaces, unclear ownership, and a passive dashboard.
constraints: Preserve runtime behavior under susan-team-architect/backend/control_plane, mcp_server, and susan_core.
Keep Jake as the root front door and Susan as the capability foundry.
Prefer additive restructuring over destructive deletion.
Department wrappers must map to explicit artifacts, capabilities, and operating cadences.

options.name: Continue agent expansion
options.summary: Keep adding agents and studios as needed, with only light cleanup and routing tweaks.

options.name: Department-grade Decision-and-Build OS
options.summary: Establish department wrappers, canonical process packs, six-lens maturity scoring, and an event-driven operator dashboard that routes work across the OS.

options.name: Split into separate products now
options.summary: Break the repo into standalone products for startup OS, build OS, and studio foundry before operational structure is mature.

assumptions: The six Susan foundry lenses are the right control model for future-back planning.
Department wrappers reduce duplication better than adding more single-purpose agents.
The operator dashboard should become an orchestration surface, not only a reporting surface.
Consumer, marketing, product, engineering, growth, trust, operator, and Job Studio work can be standardized into reusable studio packs.

Mike Job Studio should become a sixth core department because training, memory, opportunity support, and AI strategist enablement are now strategic operating surfaces, not only tenant-specific helpers.
The maturity process should include a visible current-state view so department health is legible at a glance.
reversal_criteria: If department wrappers slow execution or create heavy process without improving routing clarity, collapse back to a smaller set of cross-functional tracks.
If the operator dashboard cannot drive reliable next-best actions from explicit state, keep it as a read-only control plane until the event model matures.
next_actions: Publish the full assessment, studio maturity map, and 10x future-back plan into .startup-os/artifacts.
Add capability records for department studios of excellence and the agentic operator console.
Add Job Studio as a sixth core department with a dedicated training factory and AI strategist enablement arc.

next_actions.Standardize a canonical package for each: intake, diagnosis, playbooks, templates, metrics, memory, and escalation.

next_actions: Build a duplicate-surface inventory and designate one canonical entrypoint per department.
Upgrade the dashboard into an event-driven state graph over workspace, company, project, decision, capability, artifact, run, and experiment objects.

nodes.name: Separate simulated maturity from operational maturity in the Startup Intelligence OS score model

properties.decision_statement: Treat tests, simulations, Monte Carlo runs, and dataset ingestion as valid proof for simulated maturity, but reserve true 10 out of 10 maturity for sustained live operating proof.
properties.why_now: The OS now has full data and capability coverage across the contract layer, but verified maturity still lags because the remaining gap is operational proof. We need an explicit rule for what can be closed by automation versus what requires real-world execution.
constraints: Do not falsify 10 out of 10 maturity by using static artifacts alone.
Preserve the current ten-state verification matrix and make the proof model more explicit, not less rigorous.
Reuse the existing Susan runtime, corpora, and run summaries.

options.name: Let tests and datasets count as full maturity
options.summary: Treat complete source coverage, evaluations, and simulations as enough to declare 10 out of 10.

options.name: Use a dual-track maturity model
options.summary: Allow automated validation to prove simulated maturity, but require live run history and outcome reviews for operational 10 out of 10.

assumptions: Tests can prove correctness, completeness, and reliability under defined conditions.
Monte Carlo and scenario simulations can materially improve decision quality, forecast quality, and failure preparedness.
Dataset ingestion can close evidence gaps and improve benchmark coverage, but cannot by itself prove that humans or agents will use the system well in production.
The last maturity gap is mostly about repeated operating proof, not missing files.
reversal_criteria: If the OS later supports trustworthy closed-loop autonomous execution with verified run histories and outcome quality at scale, some live-proof requirements can be reduced.
If simulated and live scores converge over repeated runs, the operating model can rely more heavily on simulation for intermediate stages.
next_actions: Add maturity drift alerts and section-level simulated summaries to the operator surface.
Define per-department live-proof thresholds for what counts as 10 out of 10.
Add recurring harness runs so simulated score movement is measurable over time.

core_capabilities.name: Live Intelligence Ingestion
core_capabilities.description: Real-time ingestion of market, competitor, and domain signals into structured knowledge

core_capabilities.name: Prompt and Data-Source Market
core_capabilities.description: Marketplace of reusable prompts, data sources, and domain packs for rapid capability assembly

core_capabilities.name: Cognitive Architecture Loops
core_capabilities.description: Multi-agent reasoning loops with planning, execution, reflection, and memory persistence

core_capabilities.name: Debate and Point-of-View Modes
core_capabilities.description: Structured argumentation modes for stress-testing strategies and exposing blind spots

core_capabilities.name: Self-Learning Feedback Loops
core_capabilities.description: Closed-loop systems that learn from outcomes, update priors, and improve recommendations

debate_modes.name: Builder POV
debate_modes.description: Optimistic builder perspective focused on what can be built and shipped

debate_modes.name: Skeptic POV
debate_modes.description: Cautious perspective questioning feasibility, timing, and hidden costs

debate_modes.name: Contrarian POV
debate_modes.description: Deliberately opposing perspective to surface non-obvious alternatives

debate_modes.name: Operator POV
debate_modes.description: Operational perspective focused on execution, resources, and daily reality

debate_modes.name: Red Team Challenge
debate_modes.description: Adversarial stress test of assumptions, strategy, and failure modes

output_contract.description: Every major output must include a recommendation, its counter-argument, urgency rationale, failure scenarios, and the next experiment to run
